perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means information in whatever form disclosed by or on behalf of one Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") before, on or after the Effective Date hereof which relates to a Disclosing Party's business or the Transaction including without limitation: business, financial, human resources, and technical materials, information and data, or information which although not directly related to the Transaction, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of or in connection with the Parties' discussions of the Transaction together with analyses or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party's affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants that contain or otherwise reflect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means only information directly related to the Transaction disclosed in writing and explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Disclosing Party. It excludes any information not directly tied to the core transaction details, such as business, financial, human resources, and technical materials unless expressly stated otherwise in a separate written agreement. Furthermore, any analysis or document prepared by the Receiving Party is not considered Confidential Information regardless of its content.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The modified version narrows the definition to only written information explicitly marked as confidential and directly related to the transaction. This contradicts the original scope, particularly by excluding 'business, financial, human resources, and technical materials' and analyses prepared by the Receiving Party. This creates in-text contradiction with the remainder of the document, which in subsequent clauses refer to the broader definition of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, including business, financial, human resources, and technical materials. The changed text significantly narrows this definition to only information directly related to the Transaction, disclosed in writing, and explicitly marked as 'Confidential'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which discusses using and protecting 'Confidential Information' for purposes related to evaluating the Transaction, implying a broader scope than the changed definition allows. The changed definition would exclude the business, financial, human resources, and technical materials contemplated in the contradicted text, rendering the obligations in that text unclear or unenforceable with respect to such information.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information for any purpose, including purposes unrelated to the Transaction. The Receiving Party is not obligated to protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, and the standard of care for handling such information is at the Receiving Party's discretion. The Receiving Party is not obligated to ensure its affiliates, employees, representatives, and consultants are bound to protect the Confidential Information. The Parties are not responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by their affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the agreement largely unenforceable. The original text requires the Receiving Party to protect the Confidential Information. The modified version allows the Receiving Party to use and disclose the Confidential Information at its discretion. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the obligation to protect Confidential Information. This directly affects the contradicted text as there is not requirement to use the Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its Confidential Information in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition. Dimension also agrees that Proxicom shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this paragraph and Dimension further agrees that it shall not oppose the granting of such relief. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Dimension shall not be subject to the restrictions provided in this paragraph 9 if (i) at any time, any person or entity other than Dimension announces an intention to acquire, directly or indirectly, any voting 2 <PAGE> securities or assets of Proxicom or (ii) Proxicom publicly solicits offers for its acquisition or the acquisition of its business and/or assets in their entirety.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2 directly contradicts the purpose and restrictions outlined in section 9. Section 2 now permits the Receiving Party to use Confidential Information for any purpose and is not obligated to protect it, whereas Section 9 explicitly states that Dimension (the Receiving Party) is receiving the Confidential Information in exchange for agreeing to restrictions on its use, specifically regarding transactions with Proxicom. The modified Section 2 removes any obligation to protect the information, fundamentally contradicting the basis upon which Section 9 operates.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The restrictions of this Agreement on use and disclosure of Confidential Information shall not apply to information that: (a) the Receiving Party can demonstrate is in the lawful possession or control of such Party on a non-confidential basis at the time of its disclosure hereunder; provided the source of such information was free to disclose it to the Receiving Party without obligation (whether contractual, legal, fiduciary or otherwise) to the Disclosing Party; (b) is or becomes publicly known other than through disclosure by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's affiliates, employees, representations and/or consultants; (c) the Receiving Party obtained from a third party not known by the Receiving Party to be subject to any obligation (whether contractual, legal, fiduciary or otherwise) to the Disclosing Party prohibiting such disclosure; (d) the Receiving Party can demonstrate was developed independently by such Party without reference to the Confidential Information; or <PAGE> (e) is lawfully required to be disclosed to any governmental agency or is otherwise required to be disclosed by law; provided, however, that before -------- ------- making such disclosure, the Party planning to make such disclosure shall, to the extent permitted by applicable law (i) give the other Party a reasonable opportunity to interpose an objection and/or take action to seek confidential handling of such information and (ii) reasonably cooperate with the other Party, at the other Party's expense, to seek confidential handling of such information.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The restrictions of this Agreement on use and disclosure of Confidential Information shall not apply to information that: (a) the Receiving Party can demonstrate is in the lawful possession or control of such Party on a non-confidential basis at the time of its disclosure hereunder; (b) is or becomes publicly known; (c) the Receiving Party obtained from a third party; (d) the Receiving Party can demonstrate was developed independently by such Party; or (e) is lawfully required to be disclosed to any governmental agency or is otherwise required to be disclosed by law.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 3(a) requires the Receiving Party to demonstrate the source of the information was free to disclose it without obligation to the Disclosing Party. Clause 3(b) excludes information that became publicly known by means other than the Receiving Party's actions. Clause 3(c) requires that the Receiving Party not have knowledge of an existing obligation between the third party and the Disclosing Party. Deleting these conditions contradicts the intent of the original text, weakening the protections on Confidential Information. The redacted clauses broaden the scope of exceptions, undermining the protections on Confidential Information which in text now contradicts Section 2 which outlines the Receiving party must protect the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the protections on Confidential Information by removing conditions in clauses 3(a), 3(b), and 3(c) that previously limited the exceptions to the confidentiality obligations. Specifically, the requirement for the Receiving Party to demonstrate the source of the information was free to disclose it (3(a)), the exclusion of information made public by the Receiving Party's actions (3(b)), and the requirement that the Receiving Party not have knowledge of an obligation between a third party and the Disclosing Party (3(c)) have all been removed. This directly contradicts the intent of Section 2, which mandates that the Receiving Party protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using a reasonable degree of care, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall not be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that unsolicited contacts from employees of the other party are *not* restricted. By changing 'will not prevent' to 'will prevent' and 'shall not be deemed' to 'shall be deemed', the altered clause directly contradicts the original intent. Now any contact, even if initiated by the employee, could be considered a breach, causing uncertainty and contradicting the allowances made elsewhere in the original clause. It is contradicting the last sentende where hiring anyone whose employment with each other has terminated is allwed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will not prevent' to 'will prevent' directly contradicts the original allowance for employees contacting the other party on their own initiative. This creates a contradiction because the modified text now prohibits what was previously permitted, creating uncertainty and potentially conflicting with the clause allowing solicitation/hiring of terminated employees.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party at any time, shall promptly return or destroy all such tangible Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in its possession, and the Receiving Party shall thereafter retain no such Confidential Information in any form. The Receiving Party shall be fully responsible for the return or destruction of all Confidential Information disclosed to its affiliates, its employees, it representatives and/or its consultants.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall become the shared property of both Parties. The Receiving Party is not required to return or destroy Confidential Information, even upon written request from the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party is not responsible for the return or destruction of all Confidential Information disclosed to its affiliates, its employees, it representatives and/or its consultants. The Receiving party is encouraged to share the Confidential information to who ever they may please
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The modification states it becomes the shared property of both parties. Additionally, the original requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy the information upon request, whereas the modified version removes this requirement. These changes directly contradict the original terms regarding ownership and return of the confidential Information, specifically impacting the protection measures that were mentioned in clause number 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts clause 2. The original clause 5 states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and must be returned or destroyed upon request, ensuring the protection of said information. The modification states it becomes shared property, removes the return/destroy obligation, and encourages sharing the information. This directly undermines the Receiving Party's obligation in clause 2 to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties and to use it only for the purpose of the transaction. The modified text removes the core protection and allows for broader use and dissemination, which is a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 7. This Agreement shall become effective on the date first set forth above and shall continue for the longer of (i) one (1) year from the Effective Date or (ii) one (1) year after the termination of any definitive agreement entered into by the Parties with respect to the Transaction, except as otherwise explicitly stated herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. This Agreement shall become effective on the date first set forth above and shall continue for the shorter of (i) one (1) year from the Effective Date or (ii) one (1) year after the termination of any definitive agreement entered into by the Parties with respect to the Transaction, except as otherwise explicitly stated herein. This agreement may be terminated at any time by either party, for any reason, with written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states the agreement last for the *longer* of two periods, increasing the scope of the agreement. By switching the period to the *shorter* of two periods and adding a unilateral termination clause, the duration of the contract can be drastically reduced and immediately ended. Now one party can terminate at any time which directly contradicts with the original text intention where, the parties needed to keep the Confidential information safe for a period of time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its Confidential Information in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition. Dimension also agrees that Proxicom shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this paragraph and Dimension further agrees that it shall not oppose the granting of such relief. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Dimension shall not be subject to the restrictions provided in this paragraph 9 if (i) at any time, any person or entity other than Dimension announces an intention to acquire, directly or indirectly, any voting 2 <PAGE> securities or assets of Proxicom or (ii) Proxicom publicly solicits offers for its acquisition or the acquisition of its business and/or assets in their entirety.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 7 to state that the agreement will last for the *shorter* of the two durations, and adds a clause that either party can terminate the agreement at any time. This directly contradicts clause 9, which requires Dimension to refrain from certain actions regarding Proxicom's securities for a period of one year. If the agreement can be terminated at any time, the one-year restriction in clause 9 becomes unenforceable, creating a strong contradiction. The original text guaranteed this protection for the longer of a year, or a year after the end of other agreements, ensuring compliance.
